commentary: i'd just like to commend a few things about this firstly you notice the buddha was enlightened at the age of about 35 and at that age he was comparatively young there were many brahmins and external renuncians who were much older than the buddha at that time and because the buddha was young compared to them they expected the buddha to pay homage to them respect them and offer them a seat etc but the buddha did not know these and some of them got annoyed like this brahmin but you notice the buddha said brahmin whom the tata gather should salute rise up for or to whom he should offer a seat thoroughly his head would split in 2 because of that the buddha knew that nah they did not deserve his respect so i mean he's paying homage so he did not salute them pay homage to them and then the other thing you notice was the way the buddha described how he was enlightened he noticed you know that he said that he attained the janas the 4 janas 1 by 1 he attained the 4 janas and using the 4 janus firstly he recollected the past lives without the jana's the states of deep concentration he would not be able to recollect the past lives so the first 1 he recollected his past lives and when he recollected the past lives he knew that before uh in a previous life he was a monk disciple of the buddha kasapa and from the buddha kasapa he learned the 4 noble truths so you can imagine if he had not attained the the janas and recollected the past lives all the dharma they had learned before he could not remember but because he could remember his past lives all the dhamma that he learned before came back to him all the 4 noble truths etc and then secondly with the deva vision he saw the passing away and re-arising of beings according to kama and then thirdly he reflected on the 4 noble truths that he had learned in his previous life and then he destroyed the asavasa these are called the 3 knowledges so just now those those 3 were called the 3 knowledges of t vijay and so you can see that the psychic powers and the the buddha use the psychic powers of recollection of past life and deva vision and use them to attain to the third knowledge of the destruction of the asavasa the 4 noble truths and the destruction of the asava so you can see here that psychic powers have a use in the holy life that is why and the buddha actually paid great emphasis on on these 6 things that he calls the 6 arbenia the higher knowledges the higher knowledges things like this recollection of past life deva vision deva hearing the ability to read another person's thoughts and other various psychic powers like floating in the air etc and and the last 1 the sixth 1 is uh destruction of the asavasa so these you find in the sutas the buddha put the 6 knowledges as together usually in other words the the he described the other 5 psychic powers together with the destruction of the azerbaijas in the same category and you can see from here why to the buddha they are important because they are stepping stones to enlightenment if a person can attain the psychic powers that means the person's concentration is so deep that the um wisdom can come very easily and when that person uses the psychic powers in the right way not for showing off not for becoming famous or anything but like here like the buddha has described how he used the psychic powers in the right way and to attain the destruction of the asavas and then they can be seen to be very useful and they should not be despised at all you can see from the sutas they are frequently mentioned by the buddha the next 2 thai is
sutta: 8.2.11 once the exhaust yeah so uh once the exalted 1 was dwelling near varanja at the foot of naleros nimba tree there came to him a brahmin of varanja who after exchanging the usual complementary greetings sat down at 1 side so seated the brahmana spoke dust to the exalted 1 master gotama i have heard that the recluse guatemal neither salutes nor rises up for nor offers a seat to venerable and aged brahmins who arrive in years old and have attained to seniority and this is precisely the case master gautama master gautama does not salute parable and aged brahmanas this is not right master gotama and the buddha said brahman i see no 1 in the world of gods with its maras and brahmas or in the world of mankind with its recluses and brahmanas devas and men whom i should salute rise up for or to whom i should offer a seed moreover brahmin whom the tata gather should salute rise up for or to whom he should offer a seat thoroughly his head would split in 2 and the brahman said master gautama lacks stays and the buddha said this is the reason brahmin by which 1 would be right in saying the recluse gotama likes taste brahmin tastes for forms taste for sounds taste for smells taste for flavors and taste for things tangible these are abandoned by the tata gata cut off at the root made like palm tree stumps made such that they have ceased to be so that they cannot grow up again in the future this is indeed the reason brahmin by which 1 would be right in saying the recluse gotama lacks taste but not for sooth in the way you mean and the brahman said master gautama is against enjoyment this is the reason brahmin by which 1 would be right in saying the recruits gotama is against enjoyment brahmin the enjoyment of forms the enjoyment of sounds the enjoyment of smells the enjoyment of flavors and the enjoyment of things tangible these by the tata gata are abandoned cut off at the root made like palm tree stumps made such that they have ceased to be so that they cannot grow up again in the future rightly 1 might say the recluse gotama is against enjoyment but not in the way you mean and the brahman said master gautama affirms the theory of inaction and the buddha said brahmin i declare abstinence from action in respect to misconduct indeed word and thought i proclaim inaction in respect of all evil and sinful conditions rightly 1 might say the recluse gotama affirms the theory of inaction but not in the way you mean again the brahman said master gautama is an annihilationist and the buddha said brahmin i declare the cutting off of lust hatred and delusion i proclaimed the annihilation of all evil and sinful conditions rightly 1 might say that recruits gautama is an annihilationist but not in the way you mean the brahman said master gautama feels about abhorrence and the buddha said brahman i have poor misconduct indeed word and thought i applaud entertaining all evil and sinful conditions rightly 1 might say the recluse gautama feels abhorrence but not in the way you mean the brahmana said master gautama is an abolitionist and the buddha said brahman i preached the doctrine of abolishing lust hatred and delusion i teach the dhamma of abolishing all evil and sinful conditions rightly 1 may say the recruits guatemala is an abolitionist but not in the way you mean the brahmana said master gautama is given to mortification and the buddha said brahman have proclaimed that evil and sinful conditions should be mortified yes is conduct indeed word and thought verily whosoever has abandoned rooted out made like palm tree stumps has caused not to be as so conditioned that they rise not again evil and sinful conditions which should be mortified he i say is given to mortification brahman the tata gata has abandoned rooted out all such conditions rightly 1 may say the recluse gautama is given to mortification but not in the way you mean the brahmana said master gautama is against rebirth and the buddha said this is the reason brahmin by which 1 would be right in saying that recruits gotama is against rebirth verily whosoever has abandoned cut off at the root for the future descend into the womb renewed becoming and rebirth he i say is against rebirth the tatar gatherer brahmin has all done this is indeed the reason brahmin by which 1 would be right in saying the recluse gotama is against rebirth but not in the way you mean brahmin it is just as if a sitting of 8 or 10 or a dozen hens eggs were fully set upon fully warmed and fully made become and of those young chicks the 1 which before all the rest broke the eggshell with its spike claws or with its beak and hatched out safely how would you term it the oldest the the eldest or the youngest 1 would say it was the eldest master gautama certainly master gautama is the eldest of them even so brahmin when i for the sake of mankind enveloped in ignorance as it were egg born had broken the eggshell of ignorance i alone in the world was fully awake to unsurpassed and complete awakening verily brahman i was the eldest and the best in the world moreover brahmin i was resolute full of energy active upright intent and not forgetful tranquil within my being unperturbed collected and composed in mind apparently brahmin aloo from sen's desires aloo from unwholesome states i entered and abort in the first jhana which is accompanied by applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of solitude with the stilling of applied and sustained thought i entered and abort in the second jhana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of concentration with the fading away of delight i entered and aboard in the third jhana equanimous mindful and aware and i experience in my body the pleasure whereof the aryans declare the equanimous and mindful as a joyful abiding by the abandoning of bodily pleasure and bodily pain by the passing away of joy and grief i entered and abort in the fourth jhana a state of utter purity of mindfulness and equanim free of pain and pleasure with heart thus serene purified cleansed without blemish devoid of defilement supple ready to act firm and imperturbable i bend the mind so that i might know and call to mind previous lives i call to mind diverse former lives that is to say 1 birth or 2 or 3 or 4 or 5 or 10 or 20 or 30 or 40 or 50 or 100 or a thousand or 100 000 or many an age of rolling on or many an age of rolling bank or many an age of both rolling on and rolling back such 1 i was by name of such a clan of such a cast such was my food such my experience of wheel and war such was the end of my life passing away dance i arose in such a place there such was my name such my clan such my car such my food such my experience of wheel and wall such my end passing away from there i arose here thus many a previous life are called to mind with his circumstances and details this brahmin was the first knowledge attained by me in the first watch of the night ignorance being removed knowledge arose darkness being removed light arose as i abroad strenuous earnest and residue this brahmin was my first hatching like a young chicks from the eggshell with hard vaseline purified cleansed without blemish devoid of defilement supple ready to act firm and imperturbable i bend the mind so that i might discern the passing away and reappearance of beings with the deva i purified and surpassing the human eye i saw and i knew beings in course of hearing on being slowly and exalted beautiful and ugly happy and in war according to their deeds and i thought these worthies were given over to evil practices indeed word and thought will be violence of the aryans holders of wrong views men who have acquired this karma from wrong views and on the breaking up of the body after death have arisen in the untoward way the ill way the abyss hell but these good souls behave rightly indeed word and thought where no revilers of the aryans held right views and have acquired this karma therefrom and on the breaking up of the body after death have arisen in the blissful heaven world thus with the deva eye i saw a new beings in course of airing on this brahmin was the second knowledge attained by me in the second watch of the night ignorance being removed knowledge arose darkness being removed light arose as i brought strenuous earnest and resolute this brahmin was my second hatching like a young chicks from the egg shell with heart thus serene purified cleansed spotless devoid of defilement supple ready to act firm and imperturbable i bend the mind so that i might discern the destruction of the asavas in its full essence i comprehended the thought this is dukkha in his full essence i comprehended the thought this is the origin of dukkha in his full essence i comprehended the thought this is the ending of dukkah in his full essence i comprehended the thought this is the way leading to the ending of dukkha in its full essence i comprehended the thought these are the asavas in its full essence i comprehended the thought this is the origin of the arts of us in its full essence i comprehended the thought this is the ending of the asavas in his full essence i comprehended the thought this is the way leading to the ending of the asabas knowing this and seeing this my heart was freed from the asava of sensuality my heart was freed from the asava of becoming my heart was freed from the asava of ignorance in my release there came the knowledge of that release i knew both is destroyed lived is the holy life done is what must be done for me there is no more hereafter this brahmin was the third knowledge attained by me in the third watch of the night ignorance being removed knowledge arose darkness being removed light arose as i abort strenuous earnest and resolute this brahmin was my third hatching like a young chicks from the eggshell when he had thus broken the brahmin of aranja said to the exalted 1 master gautama is indeed the eldest master gautama is indeed the best wonderful wonderful master gautama just as 1 might said upright that which has been overturned might reveal the concealed might . out the way to the blind might bring an oil lamp into the darkness so that those who had eyes might see the forms about them even so has dhamma been made clear in many a figure by master gautama to master gautama i go for refuge to the dhamma and to the sangha that master gautama accept me as a lay disciple as 1 who has found refuge from this day to life's end that's the end of the sutta
